reasoning: The feat of spending 18 consecutive weeks at #1 on the Billboard 200 is historically extremely rare and practically unprecedented in the modern streaming era. Even album juggernauts like Taylor Swift's 'The Tortured Poets Department' (15 non-consecutive weeks, 12 consecutive) and Morgan Wallen's 'One Thing at a Time' (16 consecutive at most, 19 total in 2023) could not manage an 18-week unbroken streak, due to inevitable massive debut weeks from other A-list releases. Given this structural hurdle, combined with the uncertainty surrounding the actual title, release, and reception of the album itself, the probability of reaching 18 *consecutive* weeks at #1 is extremely close to zero, reflecting the market freeze value.
